refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant stakeholders.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS guarantees.
Deploy real-time packet loss monitoring and alerting system.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
Dependency on real-time monitoring data for adaptation.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality management within the protocol layer, minimizing coupling with physical network infrastructure.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
Assumes availability of accurate real-time packet loss data.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring DP explicitly if adaptation requires feedback.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and communication protocol components responsible for teleoperation control and communication quality management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics and packet loss status.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands and tasks.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation communication logic.
Vehicle module primarily hardware-focused; low containment relevance.
Highest containment score; logical place for communication protocol and packet loss management.
Safety services may consume alerts but do not manage communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if packet loss impacts safety alerts.
Dependency on Network Link interface quality and availability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface contracts with Safety Services and Network Link to mitigate risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with a clear mechanism to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if communication protocol logic is partially implemented outside Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor Teleoperation module, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry interface and validating against customer limits.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface contracts between Teleoperation and Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
Include real-time monitoring sub-FR and corresponding DP in next decomposition level.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, adding explicit sub-FRs for monitoring and reporting packet loss will improve clarity and traceability.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant stakeholders.

revise.why: To explicitly capture monitoring and reporting functions that support the main FR.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement adaptive communication protocols.
evidence_needed: Performance data on packet loss under adaptive protocol.
Interface specifications between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
